documentation.value: Retrieves rows from one or more tables. Supports filtering, grouping, ordering, and limiting results.
Retrieves only distinct (unique) rows from a query result, eliminating duplicate records.

completions.label: SELECT * FROM
completions.detail: Select all columns from a table

documentation.value: Retrieves all columns from the specified table.
Inserts one or more rows into a table with specified column values.
Inserts rows into a table using the result of a SELECT statement.
Modifies existing rows in a table that match an optional WHERE condition.
Removes rows from a table that match the specified WHERE condition. Omitting WHERE deletes all rows.

completions.label: CREATE TABLE

documentation.value: Creates a new table with the specified columns, data types, and constraints.

completions.insertText:
CREATE TABLE ${1:table_name} (
	${2:id} INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
	${3:column_name} ${4:VARCHAR(255)} ${5:NOT NULL},
	CREATED_AT TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
)

completions.label: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS
completions.detail: Create table if it doesn't exist

documentation.value: Creates a new table only if a table with the same name does not already exist.

completions.label: CREATE INDEX
completions.detail: Create an index on a table

documentation.value: Creates an index on one or more columns to improve query performance.
Creates a unique index ensuring no duplicate values in the indexed columns.

completions.label: CREATE VIEW

documentation.value: Creates a virtual table based on a SELECT query. Views simplify complex queries and provide abstraction.
Creates a new database with the specified name.

completions.label: ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN
completions.detail: Add a column to a table

documentation.value: Adds a new column to an existing table with the specified data type and constraints.

completions.label: ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN
completions.detail: Remove a column from a table

documentation.value: Removes an existing column from a table. This is a destructive operation.
Changes the data type or constraints of an existing column.
Renames an existing table to a new name.
Permanently removes a table and all its data from the database.
Removes an index from the database.
Permanently removes a database and all its tables.
Filters rows based on a specified condition. Used with SELECT, UPDATE, and DELETE statements.
Returns rows that have matching values in both tables.
Returns all rows from the left table and matched rows from the right table. Non-matching right rows are NULL.
Returns all rows from the right table and matched rows from the left table. Non-matching left rows are NULL.
Returns all rows from both tables with NULLs where there is no match.
Returns the Cartesian product of both tables — every combination of rows.

completions.label: SELF JOIN

documentation.value: Joins a table to itself using aliases, useful for hierarchical or comparative queries.
Groups rows sharing a value in specified columns into summary rows. Commonly used with aggregate functions.
Filters groups created by GROUP BY based on aggregate conditions. Similar to WHERE but for groups.
Sorts the result set by one or more columns in ascending (ASC) or descending (DESC) order.
Restricts the number of rows returned by a query.
Skips the specified number of rows before returning results. Used with LIMIT for pagination.
Combines LIMIT and OFFSET for pagination. LIMIT controls page size, OFFSET controls starting position.
Combines the result sets of two or more SELECT statements, removing duplicates.
Combines the result sets of two or more SELECT statements, including duplicates. Faster than UNION.
Returns TRUE if the subquery returns one or more rows. Often used in WHERE clauses.

completions.label: IN
completions.detail: Match against a list of values

documentation.value: Tests whether a value matches any value in a list or subquery.
Tests whether a value does not match any value in a list or subquery.
Filters values within a specified inclusive range. Works with numbers, dates, and strings.
Filters rows by pattern matching. Use '%' for any sequence of characters and '_' for a single character.

completions.label: CASE WHEN

documentation.value: Evaluates conditions and returns a value when the first condition is met, similar to IF-THEN-ELSE.
Creates an alias for a column or table, making the output more readable.

completions.label: WITH (CTE)

documentation.value: Defines a temporary named result set (CTE) that can be referenced within the main query. Improves readability for complex queries.

completions.label: WITH RECURSIVE (CTE)

documentation.value: Defines a recursive CTE for querying hierarchical or tree-structured data.
Eliminates duplicate rows from the result set.
Tests whether a value is NULL.
Tests whether a value is not NULL.
Returns the number of rows matching the query. COUNT(*) counts all rows; COUNT(column) counts non-NULL values.
Returns the total sum of a numeric column.
Returns the average value of a numeric column, ignoring NULLs.
Returns the smallest value in a column.
Returns the largest value in a column.
Concatenates values from multiple rows into a single string, with an optional separator. MySQL-specific; use STRING_AGG in PostgreSQL.
Concatenates two or more strings into one.
Returns a portion of a string starting at a specified position for a given length.
Removes leading and trailing whitespace (or specified characters) from a string.
Converts all characters in a string to uppercase.
Converts all characters in a string to lowercase.
Returns the number of characters in a string.
Replaces all occurrences of a substring within a string with a new substring.
Returns the first non-NULL value from the argument list. Useful for providing default values.
Returns the first argument if it is not NULL; otherwise returns the second argument. MySQL-specific.
Converts a value from one data type to another.
Returns the current date and time as a DATETIME value.
Returns the current date and time. Equivalent to NOW() in most databases.
Adds a specified time interval to a date.
Returns the number of days between two dates.
Extracts a specific part (YEAR, MONTH, DAY, HOUR, etc.) from a date or timestamp.
Formats a date value according to the specified format string. MySQL-specific.
Assigns a unique sequential integer to each row within a partition, starting at 1.
Assigns a rank to each row within a partition. Rows with equal values receive the same rank, leaving gaps.
Assigns a rank to each row within a partition. Equal values receive the same rank with no gaps in ranking.
Returns the value of a column from the next row (or N rows ahead) within a partition.
Returns the value of a column from the previous row (or N rows behind) within a partition.
Distributes rows into a specified number of approximately equal groups.
Defines the window (set of rows) for a window function. Can include PARTITION BY and ORDER BY.
Divides the result set into partitions for window function calculations.
Defines a column as the primary key, ensuring uniqueness and creating a clustered index.
Defines a foreign key relationship between two tables, enforcing referential integrity.

completions.label: CONSTRAINT FOREIGN KEY

documentation.value: Defines a named foreign key constraint with optional ON DELETE and ON UPDATE actions.
Ensures all values in a column or set of columns are unique.
Ensures a column cannot contain NULL values.
Sets a default value for a column when no value is specified during INSERT.
Defines a condition that each row must satisfy.
Starts a new database transaction. Changes are not committed until COMMIT is called.
Saves all changes made during the current transaction to the database.
Undoes all changes made during the current transaction.
Creates a point within a transaction to which you can later roll back.

completions.label: TRUNCATE TABLE
completions.detail: Remove all rows from a table

documentation.value: Removes all rows from a table quickly without logging individual row deletions. Cannot be rolled back in some databases.
Displays the execution plan for a query, useful for performance optimization.
Grants specific privileges on database objects to users or roles.
Removes previously granted privileges from a user or role.
A SELECT statement nested inside another statement. Can be used in WHERE, FROM, or SELECT clauses.

completions.label: CORRELATED SUBQUERY

documentation.value: A subquery that references columns from the outer query. Evaluated once per row of the outer query.
Used with CREATE or DROP to manage indexes which speed up data retrieval.

completions.label: CREATE PROCEDURE

documentation.value: Creates a stored procedure that can be called to execute a predefined set of SQL statements.

completions.insertText:
CREATE PROCEDURE ${1:procedure_name}(${2:parameters})
BEGIN
	${3:-- SQL statements}
END

completions.label: CREATE TRIGGER

documentation.value: Creates a trigger that automatically executes a set of SQL statements when a specified event occurs on a table.
Template for using aggregate or ranking functions as window functions with PARTITION BY and ORDER BY.
